candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows conv=100% but green_matches=0 and hist_hr is 0%, the signal is structurally unvalidated and must be sized down or vetoed despite the conviction reading.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: conv=100% measures live indicator agreement (how many of 30 point the same way right now), but green_matches=0 means no historical analog exists for this exact configuration — and hist_hr=0% confirms the few analogs that did exist lost. High live agreement with zero validated precedent is the signature of a crowded directional read into a catalyst (NFP morning), where institutional flow reverses on the print. EURUSD (conv 100% SHORT, hist_hr 0%) and USDJPY (conv 100% LONG, hist_hr 0%) are exactly this trap; GBPUSD (conv 100%, hist_hr 67%) and AUDUSD (conv 100%, hist_hr 72%) are validated and tradeable.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction >= 1.0 and green_matches == 0 and hist_hr < 0.40: size *= 0.0  # veto. elif consensus_conviction >= 1.0 and hist_hr >= 0.60: size *= 1.0  # full
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Win rate +3-5% and drawdown reduction by filtering EURUSD/USDJPY-type zero-precedent signals; contributes toward Sharpe gap by removing negative-expectancy fills (target +0.02-0.03 Sharpe).
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Live consensus snapshot shows EURUSD SHORT and USDJPY LONG both at conv=100% but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, while GBPUSD (hist_hr=67%) and AUDUSD (hist_hr=72%) at identical conviction have strong historical hit rates — conviction alone does not separate winners from losers; hist_hr does.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in last 7 days with Regime=UNKNOWN and an NFP print at days_until=0 — the system is correctly in a low-conviction hold; no decision logs exist yet so this lesson is harvest/consensus-grounded only.
